# PV model validation vs NREL PVWatts v8 (PySAM)

*(generated by `tools/validate_pv.py` — KDD_READINESS #8 / submission-plan WS-B)*

## Setup

- **Model under test:** `v2b_syndata.load_pipeline.pv_model.pv_ac_series`
  (isotropic transposition, Spencer solar position, PVWatts-v5 NOCT cell
  temperature, lumped DC→AC derate, inverter clip at DC/ratio).
- **Reference:** NREL **PySAM 7.1.1.post1 `Pvwattsv8`** (SAM SDK) fed with the
  **identical TMYx EPW** file — weather is byte-identical, so all differences
  are PV physics, not resource data. (The PVWatts v8 web API was therefore not
  needed; no NSRDB-vs-TMYx weather delta to attribute.)
- **Station:** `USA_CA_San.Jose-Mineta.Intl.AP.724945_TMYx` (lat 37.359, lon −121.924, tz −8), full typical year
  indexed as 2021.
- **Configuration:** DC 100 kW, tilt 10°,
  azimuth 180° (due South in BOTH conventions — verified),
  DC/AC ratio 1.2, module `standard`,
  albedo 0.2, lumped system_derate 0.86.
- **SAM settings:** array_type 0 (fixed open rack, matching our NOCT-45 open-rack
  thermal model), snow loss off, bifaciality 0, fixed albedo 0.2.

### Loss-semantics mapping

Our `system_derate=0.86` is a **lumped total** DC→AC derate
(inverter included). PVWatts separates DC `losses` from the inverter
(`inv_eff` = 96% nominal + part-load curve). Two reference runs:

1. **Primary (standard PVWatts semantics):** `losses` = 14.00%,
   inverter curve active — what anyone reproducing "14% system
   losses" in PVWatts/SAM gets.
2. **Derate-equalized (physics isolation):** `losses` = 10.4167% so
   that losses × nominal inv_eff equals our lumped 0.86 — isolates
   transposition + thermal + clipping differences from loss bookkeeping.

## Time-axis check (before trusting error metrics)

Hourly cross-correlation vs lag: -3h:0.6565 -2h:0.8479 -1h:0.9698 +0h:0.9939 +1h:0.9087 +2h:0.7393 +3h:0.5197

Best lag = **+0 h** (r = 0.9939 at lag 0) —
the two series are time-aligned; no solar-time/timezone shift detected.

## Results — primary reference

| Metric | Value | Threshold | Verdict |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Annual energy (ours) | 164,468 kWh | | |
| Annual energy (PVWatts v8) | 162,403 kWh | | |
| **Annual energy error** | **+1.27%** | < ±5% | **PASS** |
| Hourly CV(RMSE) (all hours) | 15.08% | ≤ 30% (G14 hourly) | PASS |
| Hourly NMBE (all hours) | +1.27% | ≤ ±10% (G14 hourly) | PASS |
| Hourly CV(RMSE) (ref-producing hours) | 10.60% | — | |
| Hourly NMBE (ref-producing hours) | +1.27% | — | |
| Hourly Pearson r | 0.9939 | — | |

## Results — derate-equalized reference (attribution)

Annual: ours 164,468 kWh vs 169,209 kWh
→ **-2.80%**; hourly CV(RMSE) 16.06%,
NMBE -2.80%, r 0.9939.

Attribution: with total losses equalized, the remaining -2.80%
is the pure physics gap (isotropic vs Perez transposition, NOCT vs SAM cell-temperature
model, constant vs part-load inverter efficiency, our −0.35%/°C vs SAM's standard-module
temperature coefficient). Under standard PVWatts loss semantics (primary) the two
bookkeeping choices partially offset, giving +1.27% overall.

## Artifacts

- Hourly pair CSV: `docs/experiments/pv_validation_hourly.csv` (timestamp, ours_kw,
  sam_primary_kw, sam_equalized_kw).
- Regenerate: `uv run python tools/validate_pv.py`

## Verdict

Annual energy error +1.27% vs the standard-semantics NREL
PVWatts v8 reference on identical weather → **PASS** against the <5%
gate. Hourly agreement r = 0.9939, CV(RMSE) = 15.1%,
NMBE = +1.3% (within ASHRAE
Guideline-14 hourly calibration tolerances of ≤30% / ±10%).
